statement: The suite stress-test continuation is based on live PR #43 rather than a duplicate audit.
revisit_when: PR #43 closes, is superseded, or its live head differs from 03c16761d67f047b0ffb8a73b9d0b09b65045127 at consume time.

statement: Future relay dispatch for this work is gated on verified writable-checkout, test-shell, GitHub-mutation, and isolated-context capabilities.
revisit_when: A target passes all four capability checks or the operator separately reassigns execution to the origin harness.

failure_chain.prompting_event: A second target returned BLOCKED with the same connector-only capability gap.
vulnerabilities: Target selection occurred without verifying its writable checkout and execution surface.
Repository read access was mistaken for end-to-end outsource capability.
links: A READY pointer was sent to a target that could read the packet.
The target lacked outbound Git checkout and a runnable test shell.
The target lacked branch, commit, push, and pull-request mutation actions.
The target could only return another BLOCKED relay.
failure_chain.target_failure: The outsourced audit made no additional repository progress on its second relay attempt.
failure_chain.consequences: A relay turn was consumed while PR #43 remained partial and unverified.
failure_chain.earliest_interruptible_link: Verify the target harness tool surface before emitting a READY pointer.
failure_chain.replacement_behavior: Require affirmative evidence for writable checkout, test shell, authenticated GitHub mutation, and isolated contexts before dispatch.
failure_chain.rehearsal_fixture: Given a read-only GitHub connector with no shell, classify target preflight as BLOCKED and emit no execution prompt.

statement: ChatGPT outsource work for PR #43 is limited to a separate read-only source review unless its execution capabilities are independently verified.
revisit_when: A selected ChatGPT target exposes and verifies a writable checkout, runnable test shell, authenticated GitHub mutation, and isolated execution contexts, or the operator changes the boundary.

failure_chain.prompting_event: The operator stopped repeated zero-progress BLOCKED relays and required a task ChatGPT can actually perform.
vulnerabilities: The execution packet exceeded the selected target's verified tool surface.
The same capability failure was relayed back instead of selecting capability-matched work.
links: A full execution contract was prepared.
A read-only target received the contract.
The blocked state was passed back through additional relay turns without useful work.
failure_chain.target_failure: Repeated ChatGPT exchanges produced no repository analysis or implementation progress.
failure_chain.consequences: Operator time was consumed and the useful read-only review opportunity was missed.
failure_chain.earliest_interruptible_link: Match the task contract to the target's verified capabilities before emitting the prompt.
failure_chain.replacement_behavior: Send connector-only ChatGPT targets a frozen read-only PR review and reserve execution requirements for a coding harness.
failure_chain.rehearsal_fixture: Given public GitHub read access but no shell or write actions, issue a pinned source-review task that forbids runtime and mutation claims.
